bead.title: benchmark wrapper: support ad-hoc subset dry runs
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Independent verification of closed bead fizeau-7adc4c19 failed its fourth acceptance criterion. The command `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --subset terminalbench-2-1-canary --lanes fiz-vidar-ds4-mtp --dry-run` exits 2 with `unknown flag: --subset`, so operators cannot run the documented ad-hoc subset dry-run through the wrapper even though the underlying bench sweep supports it. The same error path prints shell noise like `line 136: all: command not found` because the usage heredoc evaluates backticked text.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh:198-244` parses wrapper flags but only accepts `--phase`, `--lanes`, output/tasks/plan/dry-run/prepare/budget options, and rejects unknown flags; it never forwards `--subset`. `cmd/bench/sweep.go:211-218` defines the underlying `fiz-bench sweep --subset` flag, and `cmd/bench/sweep.go:957-1001` implements the ad-hoc subset + lanes path. `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh:136-156` uses an unquoted heredoc while embedding backticks around words like `all`, `full`, and `tb21-all`, which shell-evaluates those words while printing usage.

PROPOSED FIX
Teach the wrapper to accept `--subset <id>` and `--subset=<id>`, treat it as mutually exclusive with `--phase`, display it in usage/resume output, and forward it to `fiz-bench sweep` so the ad-hoc subset path can validate profiles and print a dry-run plan. Quote or escape the usage heredoc so help/error output is clean. Add focused tests around wrapper argument behavior instead of requiring a full TerminalBench run.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh`
- A focused test file under the existing Go or shell test surface for benchmark wrapper argument parsing/dry-run behavior
- Minimal test hooks in `cmd/bench` only if needed to run the wrapper without external downloads

NON-SCOPE
- Full TerminalBench execution
- Changing recipe/subset YAML semantics
- Changing DS4 provider behavior or MTP performance claims
- Reworking benchmark result directories or generated artifacts
bead.acceptance:
1. Add a named regression test such as `TestRunTerminalbenchSweepWrapperSupportsSubsetDryRun` (or the closest existing benchmark test name) proving the wrapper accepts `--subset terminalbench-2-1-canary --lanes fiz-vidar-ds4-mtp --dry-run` and forwards an ad-hoc subset invocation instead of exiting with `unknown flag: --subset`.
2. Add a named regression test such as `TestRunTerminalbenchSweepWrapperUsageDoesNotExecuteBackticks` proving help/error output no longer emits shell command-substitution noise like `line 136: all: command not found`.
3. `go test ./cmd/bench -run "TestRunTerminalbenchSweepWrapper" -count=1` passes, or if the tests live outside Go, the bead documents and runs the exact focused wrapper test command.
4. `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --subset terminalbench-2-1-canary --lanes fiz-vidar-ds4-mtp --dry-run` reaches the dry-run plan for `fiz-vidar-ds4-mtp` without `unknown flag`, profile-not-found, or usage heredoc command-substitution errors.
5. `go test ./cmd/bench -run TestLoadSweepPlan -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
